title: hieracium praecox subsp. basalticum
intro: Hieracium praecox subsp. basalticum, also known as Basalt Hawk's Beard, is a rare, perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting liver function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, flowers, and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
